title: 'Sex and the City' in Paris: Candace Bushnell on her upcoming one-woman show
description: In this edition of Entre Nous we're pleased to speak to Candace Bushnell, creator of "Sex and the City", the book that was the basis for the hit HBO show about four New York friends – Carrie, Miranda, Charlotte and Samantha – living and dating in the Big Apple. Candace has a one-woman show coming up at the Folies Bergère in Paris on April 26. She tells us what to expect from her show.

title: What to know about the resignation of Joe Kent, counterterrorism chief and Trump loyalist
description: The head of the US's National Counterterrorism Center, Joe Kent, resigned from his post on Tuesday in opposition to the Trump administration's war on Iran. The former staunch Trump supporter said he could not support the war as "Iran posed no imminent threat" to the US.

title: To return to Gaza, Palestinian refugees first have to face Israeli-backed militias
description: Israel has said it is reopening the Rafah border crossing between the Gaza Strip and Egypt on Wednesday. Israeli authorities had partially opened the crossing on February 2 as part of the peace plan agreed with Hamas in October, but closed it on February 28 when Israeli and US missiles and planes began striking Iran.

title: Beirut building collapses amid fresh Israeli strikes in capital city's centre
description: #Israel led some of the most intense strikes against #Lebanon's capital centre since the start of the offensive on March 2. At least a dozen died, bringing up the number of people killed since early March to at least 900.

title: Trump pressures media on Iran war with claims of 'treason'
description: US President Donald Trump and his allies are attacking news organisations for their coverage of the war on Iran, a conflict the administration says is going well for the United States but is unpopular with the public and has plunged the Middle East into chaos. Trump accused unspecified “fake news media” of working with Tehran to spread AI-generated images and said they should be charged with treason.

title: Tens of millions more in acute hunger if Mideast war drags on, UN warns
description: An extra 45 million people could face acute hunger if the Mideast war carried on through June, swelling the number worldwide to a "terrible" high, the United Nations warned Tuesday. The war, now in its third week, has killed hundreds and seen Iran launch retaliatory strikes against Israel and Gulf nations, as well as a front opening in Lebanon with Israel battling Hezbollah.

title: Cuba vows 'unbreakable resistance' as US pressure mounts
description: Cuba's leader on Tuesday said the US would face "unbreakable resistance" if it tries to take over the impoverished island nation, as communist authorities scrambled to fix a nationwide electricity blackout. Cuba's government is under increasingly crushing pressure, with Washington enforcing an oil blockade and openly stating it wants to end the nearly seven-decade-old US standoff with the one-party communist state.

title: Zelensky on European tour to urge allies not to forget Ukraine
description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky on Wednesday was making his visit to Spain after a stop in London on Tuesday seen as part of an effort by Kyiv to keep the war against Russia a priority for European capitals, even as they grapple with fall-out from the US-Israeli war against Iran that is consuming the Middle East.

title: Syrian refugees flee Israeli offensive in Lebanon, returning to a Syria in ruins
description: At the Joussieh border crossing near the Syrian city of Homs, thousands of Syrian refugees are fleeing the devastating war in Lebanon between Israel and Hezbollah. Driven out by Israeli air strikes after years of exile in Lebanon, families and children wait for hours before returning to a country where 90 percent of the population is surviving in post-Assad poverty. FRANCE 24's Dana Alboz and Hussam Hammoud report.

title: Former Columbia University president Lee C. Bollinger on the importance of free speech
description: The former president of Columbia University in New York, which was at the centre of sometimes violent protests against the war in Gaza, has spoken to FRANCE 24 about the importance of free speech and how universities have to teach it. Lee C. Bollinger is president emeritus of Columbia University; he ran it until just a year before the protests broke out. The university has long been known as a bastion of freedom of expression in the United States

title: Top US official resigns over Trump's war, says Iran posed no imminent threat
description: Joe Kent, the director of the National Counterterrorism Center, announced his resignation on Tuesday, citing his concerns about the justification for military strikes in Iran and saying he “cannot in good conscience” back the Trump administration’s war. “Iran posed no imminent threat to our nation, and it is clear that we started this war due to pressure from Israel and its powerful American lobby,” Kent said in a statement posted on social media

title: Iran to hold funeral for slain security chief as it vows vengeance
description: Iran will hold a funeral Wednesday for its security chief Ali Larijani as it vowed revenge, firing off a wave of missiles at Israel after it killed the powerful figure in an air strike. A barrage of Iranian missiles killed two people near Israel's commercial hub Tel Aviv, while Gulf nations intercepted rockets and drones headed for targets including US bases in the region.

title: Iran missile fire kills two in central Israel

title: Two key figures of Iranian leadership killed: Who's next?
description: Ali Larijani was the head of Iran's Security Council and a key voice in the ear of the late Ayatollah Ali Khamenei. title: F/A-18E Super Hornet That Downed Syrian Su-22 Now Flying in Operation Epic Fury

title: Five U.S. Air Force Tankers Damaged in Iranian Attack on Saudi Air Base
description: The attack on Prince Sultan Air Base brings the total number of tankers damaged during Operation Epic Fury to six, with a seventh destroyed in the mid-air incident that claimed the lives of six crew members. A recent Iranian missile strike hit Prince Sultan Air Base (PSAB) in Saudi Arabia, damaging five U.S. Air Force []

title: US CENTCOM urges civilians in Iran to avoid naval-operated ports
description:
The US Central Command (CENTCOM) has called on civilians in Iran to “immediately avoid” all port facilities where the country’s naval forces are present.
The post US CENTCOM urges civilians in Iran to avoid naval-operated ports appeared first on Naval Technology.

title: Israel intensifies strikes on repression sites, expands outreach to Iranians as regime repression deepens (March 8-10 up
description:
The joint US–Israeli campaign is striking Iran’s military and internal security infrastructure across the country while continuing to eliminate senior regime figures. Amid the pressure, the regime installed a new supreme leader, expanded regional attacks while denying responsibility, and intensified repression, as Israel increased outreach to the Iranian public.
The post Israel intensifies strikes on repression sites, expands outreach to Iranians

title: Iranian drone and missile barrages strike Arab states across the region (March 9-10 updates)
description:
Iran and its allied militias continued launching drones and missiles across the Middle East between March 9 and 10, targeting energy infrastructure, airports, military bases, residential areas, and industrial facilities in Iraq, Saudi Arabia, Kuwait, Bahrain, Qatar, the United Arab Emirates, and Jordan.
The post Iranian drone and missile barrages strike Arab states across the region (March 9-10 updates) appeared first on FDDs Long War Journal.

title: International Union of Muslim Scholars criticizes Iranian strikes on Arab states but says Israel is main threat to regio
description:
A senior leader of the Qatar-based Muslim Brotherhood-aligned International Union of Muslim Scholars condemned Iran's recent retaliatory strikes against Middle Eastern countries while also urging Muslims to unite against a broader 'Zionist-American project.' Despite criticizing Tehran, the group remains focused on Israel as the primary adversary of the Islamic world.

title: Analysis: Lebanon war intensifies as IDF strikes harder and Hezbollah escalates attacks
description: The renewed Lebanon war shows no sign of abating. Lebanon has yet to match its stern pronouncements on Hezbollah with equally tough enforcement, with the Lebanese military openly defying Beirut’s orders to disarm the group. Hezbollah has expressed its commitment to continue fighting in deed and word, including by pledging loyalty to Iran’s new supreme leader. title: Iran war is not delaying US weapons shipments to Taiwan, officials say
description: The Iran war has not delayed shipments of weapons to Taiwan, Trump administration officials said Tuesday, despite the demands of the intense air campaign.

title: The US has several options to counter Iranian mines. These are some key assets.
description: The U.S. Navy decommissioned its only four minesweepers in the Middle East last year, but still maintains other countermine capabilities it can deploy.

title: Lebanese government tries to avoid internal strife amid Israeli bombardment
description: The Lebanese army is in no position to search for Hezbollah weapons while the country is under Israeli bombardment, one analyst said.

title: Australia deploys early-warning aircraft to the Middle East amid Iran attacks
description: Australian leaders have said the country won’t be a protagonist in Israel and U.S. combat operations.

title: Number of US troops wounded in war against Iran rises to about 200
description: The U.S. military’s Central Command said the vast majority of those wounded had suffered minor injuries and 180 troops had already returned to duty.

title: Iran’s new supreme leader was ‘wounded and likely disfigured’ in US-Israeli strikes, Hegseth contends
description: Khamenei has remained out of view since his selection on Sunday by Iran’s clerical establishment.

title: US Air Force KC-135 goes down in Iraq, CENTCOM says
description: “The incident was not due to hostile fire or friendly fire,” the release said, adding that rescue efforts are ongoing.
